title.en: Gave a Loan Without Any Paperwork? How to Still Recover Your Money

answerBox.en: A loan without documents is still a valid debt — the problem is proof, and proof can be built now: bank/UPI transfer records, witnesses, and above all a fresh written acknowledgement from the borrower (a WhatsApp "I'll return your ₹2 lakh soon" is evidence and resets the 3-year limitation). Engineer the acknowledgement first, then the ₹999 legal notice, then suit or Lok Adalat.

en.title: Engineer the acknowledgement — your case depends on it
en.summary: Get the debt admitted in writing before confrontation.
en.detail: Before any legal threat, get the borrower to **admit the loan in writing**: a WhatsApp asking "when can you return the ₹2,00,000 I gave you in March?" answered with any variant of "soon / after Diwali / give me time" is an admission. Voice notes and recorded calls (you can lawfully record your own conversations) work too. Under **Section 18, Limitation Act**, each written acknowledgement restarts the 3-year clock — old loans revive this way.

en.title: Assemble the money trail
en.summary: Transfers beat everything; cash needs circumstantial build-up.
en.detail: **Bank/UPI/IMPS records** of the transfer are near-conclusive on the principal. Pure cash: reconstruct — your withdrawal records matching the date, witnesses to the handing over, the borrower's conduct (part-payments received, interest paid — each part-payment is also a Section 19 limitation reset). List every repayment received so your claim nets correctly; inflated claims damage credibility.

en.title: Legal notice with the evidence recited
en.summary: The notice narrates the loan story the court will later read.
en.detail: The **₹999 advocate's notice** recites: date and mode of the loan, the acknowledgements (quoted verbatim with dates), part-payments, and the demand with interest and a 15-day deadline. This does two jobs: settlement pressure now, and a contemporaneous narrative exhibit later. Many borrowers — especially relatives fearing family exposure — repay or restructure at this point.

en.title: Forum: suit, Lok Adalat — not the police
en.summary: Civil recovery is the lane; criminal only in narrow cases.
en.detail: With acknowledgements in hand, sue for recovery (small causes court for modest sums; regular suit otherwise — Order 37 is unavailable without a written contract/instrument, so the acknowledgements substitute as the evidence spine). **Pre-litigation Lok Adalat** is ideal for family/friend loans — settlement with decree force, relationships less scorched (see our [friend-loan guide](/help/money-recovery/friend-not-returning-money)). Police complaints work only where deception from inception is provable (borrowed with no intention to repay — Section 420 IPC, now Section 318 BNS) or a security cheque bounces.

rows.label: Suit court fee
rows.note: State slab on the amount claimed

rows.label: Lok Adalat
rows.note: Settlement decree, fee refund if suit filed

en.footnote: For the next loan: even a two-line signed note ("received ₹X from Y on date, to repay by date with Z% interest") plus a bank transfer prevents this entire guide from being necessary. A promissory note costs ₹199 to draft.

en: bina likha padhi ke udhar kaise wapas le

en.q: Are WhatsApp chats really admissible as evidence?
en.a: Yes — electronic records are admissible with a Section 65B certificate (a routine formality your advocate handles), and courts decide loan cases on chat trails regularly. Preserve originals on the device; export with metadata rather than screenshots alone where possible.

en.q: The loan was 4 years ago. Is it time-barred?
en.a: Only if nothing reset the clock: each written acknowledgement or part-payment within a 3-year window restarts limitation from that date. This is why engineering a fresh acknowledgement is step one — a message admitting the debt today makes a 4-year-old loan enforceable for 3 more years.

en.q: He is paying ₹2,000 occasionally and calling it "adjustment". Should I accept?
en.a: Accept and document — every part-payment both reduces the claim honestly and resets limitation (Section 19, if recorded in writing or through banking channels). Acknowledge receipt in the chat naming it as part-payment of the loan, so the record compounds in your favour.

en.q: Can I charge interest on an undocumented loan?
en.a: Courts can award reasonable interest (commonly 6–12%) from the demand date even absent an agreed rate, and from the loan date where circumstances imply interest was intended. State money-lending laws cap rates for lending as business — a one-off personal loan is outside them.

en.q: He says "prove it" and denies everything. How do courts weigh it?
en.a: On preponderance of probabilities — not criminal-level proof. A transfer into his account + your demand messages + his evasive replies + no explanation of why he received the money typically outweighs a bare denial. His account statement (summonable) showing the credit is often the closing blow.

reviewer: Adv. Rohan Mehta, Bar Council of Maharashtra & Goa, MAH/2214/2011
